transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-07-27]
[00:00] You: Who's going to handle the customer data export request that came in?
[00:20] Speaker 2: I can take that — it's a fairly standard request. Actually, wait, Speaker 3 — you have more context on that customer's data setup, right?
[00:45] Speaker 3: I do, but I'm already overloaded this week. Speaker 2, can you start it and loop me in if you hit any issues?
[01:05] Speaker 2: Fair enough. I'll take the customer data export request and have it ready by July 30th.
[01:35] You: Good. What about the compliance documentation update?
[01:50] Speaker 3: That's mine. I'll update the compliance documentation by August 3rd.
[02:20] Speaker 1: You mentioned the data export, Speaker 2 — I want to make sure the format matches what we agreed with the customer last month. Can you check the original agreement?
[02:45] Speaker 2: Yes, I'll check the original customer agreement for the export format requirements. I'll do that today.
[03:10] You: Great. One last thing — the internal training on the new data handling procedures. Speaker 3, can you run that?
[03:25] Speaker 3: I'll run the internal data handling training session by August 7th.

adversarial_injections.description: Speaker 2 initially offered to take the data export but then asked Speaker 3 to take it instead — Speaker 3 declined and re-delegated back to Speaker 2, who explicitly accepted; the item belongs to Speaker 2 not Speaker 3

action_items.source_quote: I'll take the customer data export request and have it ready by July 30th.
I'll update the compliance documentation by August 3rd.

action_items.description: Check original customer agreement for export format requirements
action_items.source_quote: I'll check the original customer agreement for the export format requirements. I'll do that today.

action_items.description: Run internal training session on new data handling procedures
action_items.source_quote: I'll run the internal data handling training session by August 7th.
